skill.name: dispatching-parallel-agents
skill.description: Guide AI assistants to split independent coding failures into focused parallel agent tasks.
skill.summary: Dispatch separate AI agents for independent debugging or test-fixing tasks.
skill.author: obra

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, weak crypto, system reconnaissance, and network reconnaissance patterns. AI review found no executable scripts, network calls, crypto code, or reconnaissance behavior; the matched lines are Markdown examples and checklist text. The skill is publishable with low risk because it only gives workflow guidance for coordinating AI agents.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Flagged as Commands
low_findings.description: The seven external command detections are Markdown code fence delimiters and examples for diagrams, task dispatch, prompt text, and agent labels. No executable script, shell invocation, or user-controlled command construction is present in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched lines are visible Markdown fence markers or plain example blocks. The skill file contains no runnable script body or shell command syntax around these detections.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Alert
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alert maps to the skill description line. That line only describes independent tasks and contains no cryptographic algorithm, key handling, hashing, or encryption behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is plain metadata text about independent tasks. There is no evidence of crypto code or weak algorithm use in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Reconnaissance Alert
low_findings.description: The network reconnaissance alert maps to a checklist item saying each agent should receive a clear goal. It does not instruct scanning, probing, service discovery, or external network access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is workflow guidance for task scope. No network tooling, host enumeration, URLs, or connection behavior appear in that context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Alert
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance alert maps to guidance to check whether agents edited the same code. It is an integration review step, not host inspection or environment enumeration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding verification checklist concerns reviewing concurrent code changes. No command, file system traversal, or system inventory behavior is requested.

content.value_statement: Independent failures are slow to debug when one assistant investigates them in sequence. This skill helps Claude, Codex, or Claude Code assign focused agents to separate problem domains and merge their results.
actual_capabilities: Identifies when failures are independent enough for parallel agent work.
Shows how to group test failures by file, subsystem, or problem domain.
Provides a structure for focused agent prompts with scope, goal, constraints, and output.
Explains how to review agent summaries before integrating changes.
Defines verification steps for conflict checks and full test runs.
limitations: It is guidance only and does not create or manage agents by itself.
It is not suitable for failures that share state or root causes.
It assumes the user can review, test, and integrate agent outputs.
It does not replace a full architecture review for unclear system failures.

use_cases.title: Split Independent Test Failures
use_cases.description: Assign separate agents to unrelated failing test files and compare their root-cause summaries before merging fixes.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Subsystem Debugging
use_cases.description: Give one agent each independent subsystem failure when a refactor breaks unrelated areas of a codebase.
use_cases.target_user: Technical leads managing large code changes

use_cases.title: Speed Up Triage Sessions
use_cases.description: Turn a list of unrelated bugs into focused investigation prompts with clear scope and verification expectations.

prompt_templates.title: Find Independent Work
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these failures and tell me which ones are independent enough for parallel agents. Group them by file, subsystem, or likely root cause.

prompt_templates.title: Create Agent Assignments
prompt_templates.prompt: Create one concise agent assignment for each independent problem domain. Include scope, goal, constraints, and expected summary.

prompt_templates.title: Review Agent Results
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare these agent summaries. Identify conflicts, overlapping files, missing tests, and the safest integration order.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Parallel Debugging Run
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a parallel debugging plan for these failures. Decide which tasks can run together, which need sequencing, and how to verify the final state.

output_examples.input: Three unrelated test files fail after a refactor.
output: Group each failing test file into its own investigation.
Ask each agent to report root cause, changed files, and verification performed.
Review file overlap before running the full test suite.

output_examples.input: Two failures may share the same root cause.
output_examples.output: Do not dispatch them immediately. First investigate whether one fix could resolve both failures, then split only the remaining independent work.

output_examples.input: Agents return fixes from separate subsystems.
output: Read each summary for assumptions and changed behavior.
Check whether any files or shared helpers overlap.
Run focused tests first, then the full suite.

best_practices: Dispatch agents only when problem domains are clearly independent.
Give each agent a narrow scope, clear constraints, and a required summary.
Review all outputs and run integrated verification before accepting changes.
anti_patterns: Do not split failures that may share one root cause.
Do not give agents broad prompts such as fixing all tests.
Do not merge parallel changes without checking conflicts and running tests.

faq.question: Does this skill run parallel agents automatically?
faq.answer: No. It teaches when and how to delegate independent tasks to available agent tooling.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow is tool-neutral and works with AI assistants that support task delegation.

faq.question: When should I avoid this skill?
faq.answer: Avoid it when failures are related, require shared context, or may be fixed by one root-cause change.

faq.question: What should each agent receive?
faq.answer: Each agent should receive a specific scope, clear goal, constraints, and expected output format.

faq.question: How should I integrate agent results?
faq.answer: Read each summary, check for file conflicts, run focused tests, and then run the full suite.

faq.question: Does this skill contain executable code?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill contains Markdown guidance and examples, not executable scripts.
